chunks.text: image, after this there is a  sepia filter, so if I put a sepia filter here  suppose I say sepia  then it will become like this  you must have seen images like this

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: filters, one very important filter is  invert filter which inverts an image  so if I come on this image and I say  filter, invert  and here I make it 1, so this image will be

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: inverted, inverted image will be made  all colors will be inverted, it will be converted to negative image  if you have seen old times  images, then in that  we don't get to see these things

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: and if I reduce this value  like I reduce it to 0.1 or  0.61, so this will be inverted to a limit  so you can  invert it a little, opacity

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: filter can be applied, so opacity  filter reduces opacity  so if I say opacity  means how transparent  this image is, so I say

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: opacity, if it is 0 then image  will be gone, if it is 1 then image will be  visible, in 0 it will be  visible, so as I increase  this value, let's say 0.1

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: and as I increase  this value, let's say I make it 0.2  and 0.3, so this image  will be as thick as this, and if  there is something else below this, then that will also be visible if opacity

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: is less, so if you use  grid and overlap, one of  the most important use cases of  grid is that you  can grid the display and

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: bring all things in one cell and  stack items on top of each other, I  told you in the grid video, so if you  want to do something like that, then you can use opacity  so that the thing behind can also be seen

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: ok, rest here  there is no need to keep it,  advanced I will say this is a concept  of CSS, it is easy to use  but its usage is a little advanced

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: ok, I myself  do not find myself using a lot of filters  I am telling you my  experience, in most of  the websites, but yes sometimes the client

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: says that I want this image like this,  if you want that you do not have to go to photoshop  or any editing software  then you have built it in  CSS and its rendering is also very good

chunks.title: CSS Filters
chunks.text: so I hope you have understood this  thing, if you want to apply Gaussian blur  then use blur, brightness, contrast,  drop shadow, gray scale, hue rotate, invert  opacity, saturate, sepia, I have

chunks.text: told you all this and you do not  need to keep any of these,  now you can give space to apply multiple filters  and by giving space you can  combine filters, so that was

chunks.title: CSS Filters
